title: StringBuilder: мутабельная сборка строки в один буфер

cards.prompt: A default StringBuilder starts at Capacity 16; appending a 19-character string overflows it so the capacity doubles to 32 while Length becomes 19, printing 16 then 19 32
cards.code: using System; using System.Text; var sb = new StringBuilder(); Console.WriteLine(sb.Capacity); sb.Append("This is a sentence."); Console.WriteLine($"{sb.Length} {sb.Capacity}");

cards.prompt: StringBuilder is mutable: Append writes into a single object and returns the same instance (fluent), so ReferenceEquals(sb, same) is True and the value is abc, printing True then abc
cards.code: using System; using System.Text; var sb = new StringBuilder(); var same = sb.Append("ab").Append("c"); Console.WriteLine(object.ReferenceEquals(sb, same)); Console.WriteLine(sb.ToString());

cards.prompt: Append, Replace and Insert all mutate the one buffer in place: hello + world, l replaced by L, and prefix inserted, yielding >> heLLo worLd with Length 14
cards.code: using System; using System.Text; var sb = new StringBuilder("hello"); sb.Append(" world"); sb.Replace("l", "L"); sb.Insert(0, ">> "); Console.WriteLine(sb.ToString()); Console.WriteLine(sb.Length);
